title: node:test, Supertest, Contracts, Properties, Fuzzing, and Fault Injection · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript decisions for node:test, supertest, contracts, properties, fuzzing, and fault injection and demands the smallest safe layered deterministic server verification matrix with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for layered deterministic server verification matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use node:test suites, subtests, assertions, fixtures, hooks, concurrency, timers, and cleanup to prove domain decisions independently of Express.
Exercise Express handlers with Supertest using exact methods, paths, fields, bodies, identity, effects, errors, and cleanup without unnecessary public listeners.
Verify runtime requests and responses against schemas and consumer cases while detecting undocumented statuses, fields, nullability, security, and behavioral drift.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Pure Domain Node Test · changed case · Quiz Pure Domain Node Test
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz team handles case 5499 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate release on layered domain, handler, fuzz, and resource-lifecycle evidence; preserve response ownership after a client abort; then change the method and preserve both status and durable-effect evidence. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use node:test suites, subtests, assertions, fixtures, hooks, concurrency, timers, and cleanup to prove domain decisions independently of Express.
